name: Correctional Workers
description_short: Correctional Workers Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union serving correctional workers with basic banking, ATM access, and financial services from their El Reno, Oklahoma location.
description_long:
Correctional Workers Federal Credit Union (CWFCU) is a federal credit union chartered to serve correctional workers and their families. Based in El Reno, Oklahoma, the institution operates as a not-for-profit, member-owned cooperative regulated by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA), distinguishing it from for-profit banks. The credit union has maintained a physical presence at 406 Reformatory Circle since its establishment, serving a specialized membership tied to the correctional workforce.

CWFCU offers core consumer banking services typical of community credit unions, including online banking access, checking and savings accounts, ATM/debit card services, and audio banking for remote account management. Members can reorder checks through the institution and access fraud protection services. The credit union operates a single branch location with standard business hours (Monday-Friday, 8:30am-4:30pm, with extended Thursday hours until 5:00pm) and provides multiple contact channels including phone, email, fax, and audio banking services.

What distinguishes CWFCU is its mission-driven focus on a specific occupational membership—correctional workers—rather than serving the general public. This specialized targeting allows the credit union to tailor products and services to the unique financial needs of its niche community. The institution emphasizes member convenience through multiple access channels, including 24/7 fraud reporting capabilities and a dedicated audio banking system for account management outside business hours.

However, the website provides limited detail about product offerings, interest rates, fees, loan programs, or membership requirements. There is no information about share accounts, share certificates (CDs), personal loans, PALs (payday alternative loans), or other specialized products commonly offered by credit unions. The single-location model may limit accessibility for members outside the El Reno area, and the sparse website content suggests a smaller institution with minimal digital marketing presence.
services: Online banking platform with account access
ATM/Debit Visa cards with 24/7 lost/stolen card reporting
Audio banking system for remote account balance and transaction history retrieval
Fraud monitoring and suspicious activity alerts via text or email
pros: Member-owned, not-for-profit credit union structure with NCUA insurance protection
24/7 fraud reporting available via dedicated phone line (1-888-263-3370)
Audio banking system (888-754-1659) allows account access and management outside business hours
Multiple contact options including phone, email, and fax for member support
Online banking platform for digital account access
Check reordering service available through Main Street Checks
Fraud department proactively contacts members via text or email to confirm suspicious activity
cons: Website lacks critical information about loan products, rates, fees, and membership eligibility requirements
Single physical location in El Reno, Oklahoma limits accessibility for members in other areas
No information about share accounts, CDs, personal loans, or other savings/lending products
Limited digital presence and outdated website design may indicate smaller institution with fewer resources
Membership appears restricted to correctional workers, limiting eligibility for general population
best_for: Correctional workers employed in Oklahoma seeking basic banking and ATM services
CWFCU members needing 24/7 fraud reporting and account monitoring
Credit union members in the El Reno area preferring local, community-focused financial institution
diagnosis: CWFCU is best for correctional workers in Oklahoma seeking basic banking services from a mission-driven, member-owned credit union with strong fraud protection. The primary caveat is the severe lack of information about loan products, rates, and membership requirements on the website, which makes it impossible to fully evaluate the breadth of services or assess whether membership is available to your employment category.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
